query: Mexico Sinaloa Governor Ruben Rocha US DOJ indictment drug trafficking April 2026 charges
a_issues: Content A lacks strong primary/reliable sourcing near the top and includes low-reliability/social results (Instagram, Facebook). It also contains a likely incorrect date in one result claiming prosecutors indicted Rocha on June 3, 2026, whereas the anchored reporting and DOJ/DEA material place the unsealing/announcement on April 29, 2026. A does not clearly provide the full charge set from a highly reliable source.
b_issues: Content B does not include the primary DOJ/DEA source either, and one AP headline/result snippet introduces later developments (resign/leave of absence) rather than the core indictment facts. But the core facts in the top results are consistent.
reasoning: B better enables a correct answer because it contains multiple mainstream, generally reliable sources (Guardian, CNN, AP) that consistently state the key facts: Ruben Rocha Moya, governor of Sinaloa, was charged by the U.S. on April 29, 2026; he was indicted along with nine other current or former Mexican officials; the case involved alleged assistance to the Sinaloa Cartel in trafficking drugs into the United States; and the charges included drug-trafficking-related counts and weapons charges. CNN specifically adds useful indictment detail, including that it was a five-count indictment and involved alleged ties to the Chapitos faction. By contrast, A is weakened by noisier sourcing and a contradictory/likely inaccurate Instagram result stating June 3, 2026, which could mislead an LLM on the timing.
